basic_info.name: Wanning Surf Festival / National Surfing Championship (万宁国际冲浪赛 / 全国冲浪锦标赛) - umbrella term covering the annual cluster of surf competitions held at Riyue Bay (Sun & Moon Bay), including the National Surfing Championship, China (Hainan) Surfing Open, Hainan Provincial Surfing Championship, ISA China Cup (historical), and WSL International Wanning Corona Cero Pro (returned 2025)
basic_info.location: Riyue Bay (日月湾) National Surf Training Base, Wanning City, Hainan Province, China (between Wanning and Lingshui counties, ~18°N latitude)
basic_info.operator: Sanctioned by the State General Administration of Sport's Water Sports Management Center (国家体育总局水上运动管理中心) and Chinese Surfing Association; co-organized with Hainan Provincial Sports Bureau and Wanning Municipal Government; international events sanctioned by World Surf League (WSL) and International Surfing Association (ISA)
basic_info.opening_date: N/A (annual event series; National Surfing Championship founded 2017; Wanning International Surfing Festival/Open in operation since ~2010 - 14th edition held in 2024)

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: N/A (natural surf - left point break and beach break at Riyue Bay); note 2026 also added an artificial wave-pool national event (全国人工冲浪池公开赛) hosted at the new Riyue Bay Surf Resort/中旅逐浪度假区 in March 2026
technical_specs.pool_size: not_applicable (natural surf venue; competition area set up along Riyue Bay shoreline)
technical_specs.wave_height: Typical competition-season wave height ~0.6-2.0 m; winter swell window (Oct-Mar) regularly delivers head-high to overhead sets at the left point [uncertain - exact contest-day heights vary]
technical_specs.wave_types: Long peeling left-hand point break (primary contest peak) plus beach-break peaks; suitable for shortboard, longboard and junior divisions
technical_specs.session_length: Heat formats follow WSL/ISA/CSA standard (typically 20-30 min heats); full event waiting periods run 7-8 days per competition
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Natural left-hand point break - same latitude (~18°N) as Australia's Gold Coast; rated among the world's top 10 surf destinations and considered China's premier natural surf spot

pricing_sessions.price_range: Spectator access generally free; competitor entry fees set per event by sanctioning body (CSA/WSL/ISA) [uncertain - exact figures not published publicly]
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: National Surfing Championship divisions: Open Group, U18 Group, U15 Group across Men's Shortboard, Women's Shortboard, Men's Longboard, Women's Longboard. WSL Wanning Corona Cero Pro: Men's QS 4000, Women's QS 4000, Men's Pro Junior, Women's Pro Junior. ISA China Cup (historical): national-team format with shortboard men/women; Hainan Classic legacy 4-star ASP/QS event
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: not_applicable (athletes bring their own boards; Riyue Bay village has 160+ surf clubs offering rental for visiting public)
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: not_applicable (event series; however Wanning markets a year-round '一赛多游 / one-event multi-tour' model bundling competition viewing with tourism packages)

experience_guide.best_season: October through March is the prime competition window aligned with the NE monsoon swell. Key calendar anchors observed in 2024-2026: late March-early April (China/Hainan Surfing Open), October (Hainan Provincial Surfing Championship), early-mid December (Wanning International Surfing Open / WSL Wanning Corona Cero Pro), late December (National Surfing Championship). 2026 also added an artificial-pool national event in early March
experience_guide.best_swell_window: October to March - aligned with NE monsoon delivering long-period swell to the left point; December is the peak competition month
experience_guide.crowd_level: Heavy crowds during competition weeks - hotel rates spike, line-ups in non-contest peaks remain crowded; Wanning recorded 6.67 million tourist visits in first 8 months of 2025 with ~500,000 annual surfing-experience visitors
experience_guide.booking_method: Athlete entry through Chinese Surfing Association (CSA) for national events, World Surf League athlete portal for WSL QS/Pro Junior events, ISA national federation entry for ISA-sanctioned events; spectator viewing on-site free
experience_guide.booking_platform: WSL: worldsurfleague.com event page; CSA national events: Chinese Surfing Association official channels; local schedule announcements via Wanning Municipal Government website (wanning.hainan.gov.cn) and Hainan Provincial Sports Bureau
experience_guide.payment_options: not_applicable (spectator); athlete entry fees handled via federation/WSL portals
experience_guide.skill_required: Competitor: Advanced/Pro (selection through provincial/national qualifying); Spectator: none
experience_guide.age_eligibility: U15 (under 15), U18 (under 18), Open (adult) divisions for National Championship; Pro Junior (under 18) and Open QS divisions for WSL events
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 3 - family-friendly viewing on the beach; concurrent surf-village activities, music festivals and beach markets cater to children, but actual competition spectating requires sun/heat tolerance
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Riyue Bay has historically hosted ISA World StandUp Paddle Championships in addition to surfing events [uncertain - SUP not part of every annual edition]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: WSL events live-streamed on worldsurfleague.com; national events broadcast/streamed via CCTV-5 and provincial channels [uncertain - per-event specifics vary]
experience_guide.language_support: WSL events: English + Mandarin; national events: Mandarin primary
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: [uncertain] - no public information on adaptive surfing divisions at the Wanning festival
experience_guide.safety_notes: Sanctioned events use ISA-certified judges and on-water safety personnel (water-patrol skis, lifeguards); competition zones are coned off and recreational surfers excluded during heats
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: ANNUAL FESTIVAL CALENDAR (Riyue Bay / National Surf Training Base): (1) March-April: 中国(海南)冲浪公开赛 China (Hainan) Surfing Open - 2025 edition Mar 28-Apr 2 with 39 teams/300+ athletes; (2) March 2026 NEW: 全国人工冲浪池公开赛 National Artificial Wave Pool Open Mar 7-9, 2026 at adjacent CTG Wave Resort; (3) October: 海南省冲浪锦标赛 Hainan Provincial Surfing Championship (2024 edition concluded Oct 15); (4) Early-Mid December: 万宁国际冲浪赛 Wanning International Surfing Open / WSL International Wanning Corona Cero Pro QS 4000 + Pro Junior - 2024 edition (14th) Dec 8-15, 2025 edition Dec 11-17 (first WSL event in China since Jan 2020, prize purse not officially disclosed); (5) Late December: 全国冲浪锦标赛 National Surfing Championship - 2020 Dec 21-29, 2023 Dec 5-11, 2024 Dec 27, 2025 Dec 20-27 (36 teams, ~300 athletes). Historical: ISA China Cup + Hainan Classic ran 2014 in November. Wanning hosts ~42 sport-tourism events annually under '潮玩山海·浪动万宁' theme. PRACTICAL IMPACT: expect partial beach/lineup closures (封场) during competition heats roughly 3-4 weeks per year clustered Mar-Apr, Oct, and especially Dec; book accommodation 1-2 months ahead for Dec window

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Riyue Bay surf village hosts 130+ guesthouses/B&Bs; on-site Riyue Bay Surf Resort (中旅逐浪度假区, opened Nov 11, 2025) provides flagship contest hotel; rates spike sharply during contest weeks
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Riyue Bay Surf Resort (CTG Group / 中旅集团 逐浪度假区) - flagship surf-themed resort opened Nov 11, 2025 to anchor 2026 Hainan Marine Tourism Year [uncertain - exact room count and rates]
surroundings.nearby_food: Surf-village cafes and seafood restaurants in Riyue Bay; 160+ surf clubs many with attached F&B; hotel restaurants at Shimei Bay (石梅湾) and Shenzhou Peninsula (神州半岛) within 20-40 min drive
surroundings.transport: Self-drive via G98 Hainan Ring Expressway; new Riyue Bay interchange (日月湾互通) opened to improve access; high-speed-rail to Shenzhou or Wanning station then taxi/网约车 (~30-40 min)
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX) ~90-100 km / ~75-90 min by car; Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK) ~200 km / ~2.5 h via expressway/HSR
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Riyue Bay (contest viewing + surf village) + Shimei Bay (Nine-Mile Cultural Bay - hosts concurrent surf-music-festival events) + Shenzhou Peninsula (golf, marine sports park planned) + Xinglong Tropical Botanical Garden + Wanning city; '一赛多游' (one-event multi-tour) packages bundle competition + tourism

pros: Only national-level surf training base in China - hosts both grassroots qualifying and elite WSL/ISA events under one umbrella
Reliable winter swell window (Oct-Mar) gives consistent contestable conditions
December cluster (WSL Pro + National Championship) creates a global-attention moment for Chinese surfing
Strong government and CTG Group backing - 2025 saw return of WSL after 5-year hiatus and opening of flagship surf resort
Local surf-village ecosystem (160+ clubs, 130+ B&Bs) supports family-friendly festival atmosphere
cons: Competition weeks lock up the prime left-point lineup - recreational surfers must shift to secondary peaks
Accommodation prices and crowding spike sharply during contest windows (especially mid-late December)
Date confirmation often comes <8 weeks out, complicating travel planning
Wave conditions ocean-dependent - some heats run in marginal surf
Festival schedule fragmented across multiple sanctioning bodies (CSA/ISA/WSL/Hainan Province) - no single consolidated calendar
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: [uncertain] - no aggregated review score available; coverage is via sports media (Xinhua, China News Service, CCTV-5) and WSL editorial; local Xiaohongshu/Mafengwo notes describe heavy crowds but festive atmosphere during December competition window
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Advanced surfers and competitors (athlete entry); surf-tourism spectators and photographers (December peak); families combining surf-watch with Hainan resort vacation; sports-tourism researchers and industry attendees
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Chinese Surfing Association × State Sport General Administration Water Sports Management Center; WSL Asia Region partnership with Wanning Municipal Government; CTG Group (中国旅游集团 / China Tourism Group) operating Riyue Bay Surf Resort as official venue partner; title sponsorship of WSL event by Corona Cero (AB InBev)

verification.verification_sources: (1) WSL official event page worldsurfleague.com/events/2025/qs/476/wsl-international-wanning-corona-cero-pro/main; (2) WSL article 'Elite Competition Returns to Riyue Bay for WSL International Wanning Corona Cero Pro QS 4000'; (3) Wanning Municipal Government 万宁市人民政府 wanning.hainan.gov.cn/wanning/ywdt/jrbt/202410/ - 2024 Hainan Provincial Surfing Championship; (4) China News Service Hainan hi.chinanews.com.cn 2025-03-31 - Wanning 42 annual sport events; (5) cnpiw.cn 浪动万宁 2025年全国冲浪锦标赛隆重启幕 (Dec 20-27, 2025 National Championship); (6) Baidu Baike 全国冲浪锦标赛 entry (founded 2017, governed by State Sport General Administration Water Sports Management Center); (7) ISA isasurf.org/timeline/hainan-wanning-riyue-bay-international-surfing-festival/; (8) China Daily qiye.chinadaily.com.cn - 2026 全国人工冲浪池公开赛 Mar 7-9, 2026; (9) Xinhua news.cn 2025-08-27 - Wanning sport-tourism integration; (10) Korea Herald koreaherald.com/article/10623347 + Laotian Times - Riyue Bay Surf Resort opening Nov 11, 2025
